Load data:
```{r}
mi_features = readRDS("data/mi_features.rds")
mi_variables = readRDS("data/mi_variables.rds")
```

Different feature types:
```{r}
unique(mi_variables$type)
```

Identify numerical columns/features:Continuous, Integer and Binary:
```{r}
#selects vars according to each
mi_variables = mi_variables[
  (mi_variables$type %in% c("Continuous", "Integer", "Binary")),]

#selecting vars in the (features) data set
mi_features = mi_features[
  intersect(colnames(mi_features), mi_variables$name)
  ]
```


Different features have different units therefore we need to scale the covariance matrix:
```{r}
pca <- prcomp(mi_features, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

summary(pca)
```

The first components do not 'explain' much of the variance.
Which indicate our data is uncorrelated between observations, as shown by th correlation plot:
```{r}
R = cor(mi_features_num)
corrplot(R, method = "color", tl.pos = "n")
```
